provenance.notes: Downloaded the original Commons JPEG/PNG after checking the file page summary and licensing sections.

quality.notes: Colour photo of the manuscript opening pasted on blue mount.

rights.evidence_text: Commons file page declares CC BY-SA 4.0 by the photographer (User:Davidbena); the underlying 1876 manuscript is public domain.

title: Title page of a Yemenite Pirke de-Rabbi Eliezer manuscript (1876)

handwriting.notes: Tefilat Shacharit heading at top with daily Psalms list below in Provençal cursive Hebrew.

provenance.notes: Downloaded the original Commons JPEG/PNG after checking the file page summary and licensing sections.

quality.notes: Colour photograph of the manuscript opening.

rights.evidence_text: Commons file page tags the c.1650 leaf as public domain.

title: Provençal Hebrew cursive prayer leaf, 17th century

handwriting.notes: Tight crop showing a few large Hebrew display words with vocalisation marks and the paseq vertical bar.

provenance.notes: Downloaded the original Commons JPEG/PNG after checking the file page summary and licensing sections.

quality.notes: Very small crop (103x155 px); useful as a punctuation-context example, not as a full-page sample.

rights.evidence_text: Commons file page tags the 14th-century Pentateuch crop as public domain.

title: Crop of a 14th-century Hebrew Pentateuch leaf (paseq illustration)

handwriting.notes: Magical Hebrew handwriting with diagrams, character-bearing magical squares, and red-ruled frames around figures.

provenance.notes: Downloaded the original Commons JPEG/PNG after checking the file page summary and licensing sections.

quality.notes: High-resolution page crops from the NLI source PDF.

rights.evidence_text: Each Commons crop is tagged public domain; the underlying manuscript is Geniza-era and the text is pseudonymously ascribed to angelic dictation, with no copyright claim possible.
